Name: Rohan Sharma
What did you like most?: Live Black-Scholes pricing on-chain is impressive — premiums update in real-time and match what I'd expect from a CEX options desk. The strike grid (ATM ±20%) covers most of what I trade.
What could be improved?: Need order book / limit orders eventually. The single-strike buy flow works but pro traders want to ladder positions. Also missing Greeks (delta, gamma, theta) on the chain.
Additional Feedback: Would love a Telegram alert bot for when ATM premiums move >X%. Also: please add weekly + monthly expiries, not just weekly Fridays.

Name: Ananya Iyer
What did you like most?: Vault UX is clean — deposit/withdraw is one click and share price math is transparent. Capital utilization bar is a nice touch, I can see at a glance whether my USDC is earning.
What could be improved?: APY estimate feels theoretical. Would prefer to see realized 7-day / 30-day APR based on actual premium income. Also need a way to see historical LP returns before depositing.
Additional Feedback: Adding auto-compound or auto-reinvest would be killer. Currently shares appreciate but I have to manually compound by withdrawing and redepositing if I want more shares.

Name: Karan Malhotra
What did you like most?: Cross-contract architecture is solid — premiums computed live by PricingEngine via simulation is exactly the right design (vs. storing premiums on-chain). 92 tests is reassuring.
What could be improved?: No programmatic market-maker API yet. I want to quote two-sided liquidity on multiple strikes but there's no batched-buy or RFQ endpoint. Currently every strike needs a separate signed tx.
Additional Feedback: Add a backend WebSocket for live premium updates so I don't have to poll. Also consider Reflector → DIA migration tradeoffs — DIA is good but Reflector has tighter latency on Stellar.

Name: Dev Patel
What did you like most?: The whole protocol settles in <5 sec end-to-end on testnet. That's the killer feature — no other Stellar options protocol comes close. Cross-contract calls between PricingEngine + Vault + Market are clean.
What could be improved?: Could not find a way to monitor settlement events without polling. Need WebSocket subscriptions or at least a /events endpoint with cursor-based pagination so my arb bot can stream new positions.
Additional Feedback: For arb: please expose vault.available_capital live so I can detect when LP capacity is constraining premium pricing. Also: add put-call parity check helper somewhere visible — that's the easiest arb signal.

Name: Shreya Nair
What did you like most?: Cash settlement in USDC is exactly what I need for my hedge book — no physical delivery means I don't need to manage XLM inventory on top of my spot positions. European-style is correct for systematic hedging.
What could be improved?: Only XLM/USDC underlying limits me. I have BTC/ETH spot exposure I'd want to hedge through here. Also: 80% IV is way too high for the realized vol I'm seeing — needs to be IV surface-based, not flat.
Additional Feedback: Please add downside-only puts (deep OTM puts at -30%, -40%) for tail hedging — current ±20% strike range is too narrow for protective put strategies. Daily expiries (not just weekly) would also help.

Name: Rahul Verma
What did you like most?: Underwriting vault model is elegant — I deposit once and passively underwrite all strikes, no per-position margin management. Pool-based collateral is way simpler than CEX margin requirements.
What could be improved?: No visibility into which strikes my capital is locked against. As a covered-call writer I want to know whether my locked capital is backing 0.18 strikes (high-IV) or 0.14 strikes (low-IV) so I can size my deposit.
Additional Feedback: Per-LP attribution would be huge — show me my share of premium income broken down by strike and expiry. Also: a 'safe mode' that only writes OTM (no ITM exposure for the vault) would let conservative LPs opt out of riskier strikes.
